compressed_prompt: write song lyrics on economic disparity, all lowercase
response:
(verse 1)
the numbers climb on a glass tower screen
while the rain finds the cracks in the street
a dividend paid, a forgotten bill
the distance grows in the evening still
the same sun sets, but it don't feel the same
just a different shade in the monopoly game

(chorus)
it’s a trickle-down theory in a desert town
a shuttered storefront where the hope burned down
it’s champagne fizz in a penthouse air
and the weight of a silence on a broken stair
oh, we’re all counting, but on different scales
with some buying futures and some chasing tails

(verse 2)
the coffee’s cheap but the hours are long
a background hum to the market’s song
your portfolio blooms like a springtime tree
while my rent’s a lock and the key’s on me
we share a city but we walk different beats
you on the island, i’m in the concrete streets

(chorus)
it’s a trickle-down theory in a desert town
a shuttered storefront where the hope burned down
it’s champagne fizz in a penthouse air
and the weight of a silence on a broken stair
oh, we’re all counting, but on different scales
with some buying futures and some chasing tails

(bridge)
the gap’s not a number, it’s a cold, hard rail
a different ending to every fairy tale
it’s the price of peace, it’s the cost of sleep
it’s promises buried and buried deep

(outro)
all the capital flows, but the well runs dry
just a widening space in the corner of your eye
a system’s hum, a forgotten cry
just a widening space
till the day we…
